title: Capture State Service Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: **AI Summary**: When a capture page manages an aggregate root with child routes (e.g., `/actions/:id/evidence/new`), a lightweight `@Injectable()` state service preserves aggregate data — child collections, lookup data, and the parent entity ID — during child-route navigation. The state service uses Angular `signal()` for reactivity ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md)), is provided at the **component level** (not root), and gets a fresh instance per capture ...

examples.name: Data Flow Example 1
examples.code:
Route activates: /admin/actions/:id
  │
  ├─ ActionCaptureComponent mounts
  │    providers: [ActionCaptureState]   ← fresh instance
  │
  ├─ effect() fires → loadEntity(id)
  │    │
  │    ├─ fetch aggregate DTO from API
  │    ├─ populate form signals (name, slug) — LOCAL to component
  │    ├─ populate state.evidence, state.consultations, state.actionId — SHARED via state
  │    ├─ snapshot serverEvidenceIds, serverConsultationIds — PRIVATE to component
  │    └─ load secondary data (complianceProfileOptions → state.complianceProfileOptions)
  │
  ├─ User clicks "Add Evidence" in embedded list
  │    │
  │    └─ Router navigates to /admin/actions/:id/evidence/new
  │         │
  │         ├─ <router-outlet> activates → childActive.set(true)
  │         ├─ parent content CSS-hidden (NOT destroyed) — state instance survives
  │         ├─ ActionEvidenceCaptureComponent mounts as child route
  │         ├─ inject(ActionCaptureState) ← same instance from parent
  │         ├─ reads state.evidence() for duplicate detection
  │         ├─ reads state.actionId() for new evidence's FK
  │         │
  │         ├─ User fills form, clicks Save
  │         │    state.evidence.update(list => [...list, newEvidence])
  │         │    router.navigate back to parent
  │         │
  │         └─ <router-outlet> deactivates → childActive.set(false)
  │              parent visible again, sees updated evidence signal
  │
  └─ User clicks Save on parent
       │
       ├─ diff children: server IDs → keep ID, temp UUIDs → EMPTY_GUID
       ├─ build SaveActionRequest with diffed children
       ├─ call actionService.save(request)
       └─ create mode: navigate to edit URL; edit mode: reload entity

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: The capture state service itself has no package dependencies beyond Angular core. The capture page and child route components that use it require the following packages. See [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for setup instructions.
| `@ngx-translate/core` | `TranslatePipe`, `TranslateService` | All user-facing strings — child route labels, validation messages, notifications |
| `@nxgn-solutions/ui` | `CapturePageTitleComponent`, `TextFieldComponent`, `ConfirmationDialogService` | Toolbar, form fields, confirmation dialogs in child capture pages |
| `@ng-bootstrap/ng-bootstrap` | `NgbModal` | Modals (e.g., evidence secrets display after save) |

sections.title: When to Use
content: Use a capture state service **only when** the capture page has child routes that need access to the parent's aggregate data. If the capture page has no child route navigation, local signals on the component are sufficient — even if it has embedded lists.
| `/actions/:id/evidence/new` | **Yes** | Child route needs parent's evidence list, action ID, compliance profile options |
| `/actions/:id/consultations/new` | **Yes** | Child route needs parent's consultation list, action ID |
| `/workflows/:id` | **No** | Embedded step list but no child routes — steps managed inline |
| `/users/:id` | **No** | Flat form, no child routes — local signals only |
| `/roles/:id` | **No** | Flat form with embedded lists but no child routes |

**Key distinction**: A state service is needed when child routes consume parent data via Angular DI. An embedded list that mutates a two-way-bound `model()` signal does NOT require a state service — the parent component owns the signal directly.

sections.title: Data Flow
content: │    ├─ populate form signals (name, slug) — LOCAL to component
│    ├─ populate state.evidence, state.consultations, state.actionId — SHARED via state
│    ├─ snapshot serverEvidenceIds, serverConsultationIds — PRIVATE to component
├─ User clicks "Add Evidence" in embedded list
│         ├─ parent content CSS-hidden (NOT destroyed) — state instance survives
│         ├─ reads state.actionId() for new evidence's FK
│              parent visible again, sees updated evidence signal
├─ diff children: server IDs → keep ID, temp UUIDs → EMPTY_GUID
└─ create mode: navigate to edit URL; edit mode: reload entity

sections.title: State Service
content: The state service is a plain `@Injectable()` class with no `providedIn` — it MUST be registered in the parent component's `providers` array so Angular creates a fresh instance per component mount.
From `action-capture.state.ts` — an aggregate with two child collections and lookup data:
// ✗ WRONG — root-scoped, shared across all instances
// ✗ WRONG — BehaviorSubject instead of signal (violates ADR-0006)
// ✗ WRONG — plain mutable property, no reactivity
// ✗ WRONG — form fields in state service (these stay local on the component)
| `@Injectable()` with no `providedIn` | Must be component-scoped, not root-scoped |
| All fields are `readonly signal()` | Callers use `.set()` / `.update()` — the signal reference itself never changes |
| Default entity ID is `crypto.randomUUID()` | Pre-generates a temp ID for create mode; overwritten on edit load |
| Default collections are `[]` | Safe empty state for create mode |
| One signal per child collection | Each embedded list and child route reads its own signal |
| Include lookup data used by child routes | e.g., `complianceProfileOptions` so consultation child route has dropdown options |
| File name: `{entity}-capture.state.ts` | Colocated in the feature folder alongside the capture component ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)) |
### What Goes in State vs What Stays Local
| Child collections (evidence, consultations) | **State service** | Child routes read and update them |
| Lookup data used by child routes (compliance profile options) | **State service** | Child routes need dropdown options |
| Parent entity ID | **State service** | Child routes set FK on new items |
| Form fields (name, slug, description) | **Local signals** on capture component | Only the parent form edits them |
| Loading/saving/error state | **Local signals** on capture component | Only the parent manages lifecycle |
| Server ID snapshots (`Set<string>`) | **Private field** on capture component | Only the parent diffs on save |
| Validation computeds (nameErrors, isFormValid) | **Local computeds** on capture component | Validation is form-local |

sections.title: Usage in Capture Page
content: The parent capture component provides the state service and aliases child collection signals as local properties.
id = input<string>();  // Bound from route param via withComponentInputBinding()
// --- Aggregate children: delegated to state so child routes access them ---
// --- Server ID tracking for child diffing on save ---
/** Whether a child route (evidence/consultation capture) is active */
The `loadEntity()` method populates both local form signals and shared state signals. Secondary data (lookup lists) is loaded after the primary entity.
// Populate state service (child routes access these)
// Snapshot server IDs for diffing on save
// Load lookup data for child routes (non-critical — fail silently)
// Consultation capture will show no compliance profile options
The parent's save method maps temp client-side UUIDs to `EMPTY_GUID` so the server treats them as new records, while preserving real server IDs for existing records.
// Diff children: server IDs stay, temp UUIDs become EMPTY_GUID
await this.loadAction(this.id()!);  // Reload to show server state
this.router.navigate(['/admin/actions', result.id]);  // Navigate to edit URL
const msg = (err as { error?: { errors?: { message?: string }[] } })
### Template: Hiding Parent During Child Navigation
The parent template wraps all content in a `div` that hides when a child route is active. The `<router-outlet>` goes OUTSIDE the hidden wrapper.
<!-- All parent capture page content: toolbar, form, embedded lists -->
<!-- Embedded grids that navigate to child routes -->
<!-- Outside the hidden wrapper — renders child capture route content -->

**Key**: `[class.d-none]` hides the parent with CSS — it is NOT destroyed. The state service instance (provided on the parent) stays alive, and the child route component receives the same instance via Angular DI.
<!-- ✓ CORRECT — router-outlet outside the hidden wrapper -->
<!-- ✗ WRONG — router-outlet inside the hidden wrapper (child content hidden too) -->
<!-- ✗ WRONG — @if destroys parent, killing the state service instance -->

sections.title: Usage in Child Route
content: Child route components inject the state service to read parent data and update collections on save. They do NOT provide their own state — they consume the parent's.
From `action-evidence-capture.component.ts` — shows the full pattern including form signals, validation, edit population, duplicate detection, and state update:
import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, computed, effect, inject, input, signal } from '@angular/core';
// NOTE: No providers — uses parent's ActionCaptureState
This Evidence ID is already in use.
<!-- ... additional fields (evidence type, kind, grant types, etc.) -->
/** Route param — undefined for 'evidence/new', UUID for 'evidence/:evidenceRef' */
/** ID for new records (generated once per component mount) */
/** Existing file names for duplicate detection (excludes the one being edited) */
// Populate form from state when editing an existing evidence
id: this.evidenceRef() ?? this.newRecordId,  // Temp UUID for new items
// Replace in parent's collection (preserve status from existing)
this.state.evidence.update(list => list.map(c => c.id === evidence.id ? evidence : c));
Child routes that need lookup data (e.g., compliance profile options for a consultation) read it directly from state — no additional API call needed:
/** Compliance profile options loaded by parent, reactive */
// Template iterates complianceProfileOptions() for checkbox list

Child routes detect duplicates against the parent's in-memory collection — no server round-trip needed because all sibling items are already loaded. This differs from parent-level duplicate detection (which loads unique values from the server via the feature service).
// ✓ CORRECT — child detects duplicates from state (in-memory, already loaded)
// ✗ WRONG — child calls server for duplicates (unnecessary, data already in state)
// Don't do this — the parent already has all siblings loaded
See [Validation — Duplicate Detection](validation.md#duplicate-detection) for the parent-level pattern using server-loaded unique values.

sections.title: Routing Configuration
content: Child routes are defined as a shared `Routes` array and assigned to both the `/new` and `/:id` parent routes. Each child route uses a route param to distinguish new vs edit mode.
/** Child routes for action capture — shared by /new and :id */
// Shared children for both new and edit parent routes

**Key**: The same `children` array is used for both `/new` and `/:id`. The parent component gets a fresh `ActionCaptureState` instance in either case because it's provided at component level. See [Routing](routing.md) for full route conventions.

sections.title: Notification Rules
content: Child route components do NOT show toaster notifications — they update state and navigate back. The parent capture page owns all notifications.
| Domain command result (toggle status, rotate secret) | **Parent** capture page | `ToastrService` or `ConfirmationDialogService` |
| Validation errors | **Child** route | Inline `<small class="form-text text-danger">` in template |
| Saving spinner | **Parent** capture page | Inline `<div class="alert alert-info">` |
| Error messages | **Parent** capture page | Inline `<div class="alert alert-danger">` |
All notification messages MUST go through `@ngx-translate/core`. See [Capture Page — Notification Rules](capture-page.md#notification-rules).

sections.title: Key Rules
content: - MUST be `@Injectable()` with NO `providedIn` — component-scoped, never root
- MUST use `signal()` for all fields — not plain properties, not `BehaviorSubject` ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md))
- MUST include the parent entity ID (child routes use it as FK on new items)
- MUST include all child collections that child routes read or modify
- SHOULD include lookup data that child routes need (compliance profile options, available options)
- MUST NOT include form fields, loading state, or validation — those stay on the component
- MUST NOT include domain command handlers or API calls — the state service is a data container only
- File name: `{entity}-capture.state.ts`, colocated in the feature folder ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md))
- MUST declare `providers: [EntityCaptureState]` — fresh instance per mount
- MUST populate state in `loadEntity()` — both entity ID and child collections
- MUST snapshot server child IDs (`new Set(...)`) for diffing on save
- MUST expose `childActive = signal(false)` for hiding parent during child navigation
- MUST alias state signals as local properties: `evidence = this.state.evidence`
- MUST diff children on save: server IDs stay, temp UUIDs become `EMPTY_GUID`
- MUST import `RouterOutlet` in the component's `imports` array
- MUST NOT declare `providers` for the state service — receives parent's instance via DI
- MUST inject the state service: `private readonly state = inject(EntityCaptureState)`
- MUST use `effect()` to populate form fields from state in edit mode
- MUST generate a temp UUID (`crypto.randomUUID()`) once per mount for new items
- MUST update state on save: `state.collection.update(list => [...list, newItem])` for add, `list.map(...)` for edit
- MUST navigate back to parent after save — not to the list page
- MUST use `EnumDTO` for all classification fields per [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)
- SHOULD detect duplicates against state collections (no server round-trip needed)
- SHOULD use inline `template:` (not `templateUrl:`) — child capture forms are self-contained
- Parent wraps all content in `<div [class.d-none]="childActive()">` — CSS hide, not destroy
- MUST NOT use `@if` to conditionally render parent content — destroying the parent destroys the state service
All classification fields in child DTOs MUST use `EnumDTO` — the structured type enabling zero-transformation dropdown binding and roundtrips:
// ✓ CORRECT — EnumDTO for all classification fields (ADR-0005)
// ✗ WRONG — bare strings lose type safety
// ✗ WRONG — union types require transformation for dropdown binding
evidenceType: EvidenceType;        // can't bind to dropdown without mapping
See [Feature Service — Model Rules](feature-service.md#model-rules) for the complete EnumDTO/NamedDTO convention.

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | State sharing | `@Input()` from parent to child + modal editors | `@Injectable()` state service + child routes | Child routes can't receive `@Input()` from parent; state service bridges the DI gap ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md)) |

| Child editing | `NgbModal.open()` with `componentInstance` | Child routes via `<router-outlet>` | [ADR-0015](../../adr/0015-child-routes-over-modals-for-entity-capture.md) — deep-linkable URLs; consistent navigation model; child forms can be full-page |

| Reactivity | Mutable model objects + default change detection | Immutable signal updates + OnPush | Predictable change tracking ([ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md)) |

| Provider scope | `providedIn: 'root'` for all services | Component-level for state, root for feature services | State is per-aggregate-instance; feature services are stateless singletons ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)) |

| Duplicate detection in children | Parent passes `parentItems` via `@Input()` to modal | Child reads `state.collection()` and filters | Same data, different access mechanism — signal computed replaces input |

| Save in child | Modal calls parent method or service directly | Child updates state signal, parent diffs on aggregate save | Child has no API dependency; aggregate consistency enforced at parent level |

- [Embedded List](embedded-list.md) — Child grids within the capture page that trigger navigation to child routes
- [Feature Service](feature-service.md) — The colocated HTTP service the capture page injects
- [Validation](validation.md) — Per-field errors, duplicate detection, confirmation dialogs
- [Overview](web-overview.md) — Required packages, project structure, component conventions
- [ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md) — Enum over string constants (backend C# enums)
